metadata.title: Add kanban board + full-text search data endpoints (contracts-driven)
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: Keep pm-web published scoped as @unbrained/pm-web (not unscoped pm-web)
metadata.description: User decision (2026-05-30): pm-web stays published scoped as @unbrained/pm-web. A prior audit agent (commit ae4f607) had flipped package.json name to unscoped pm-web to match the pm- branding convention, but never claimed/published the unscoped name on npm (still E404) — which would have caused publish 404s. @unbrained/pm-web@2026.5.29-1 is already published and working; pm install github.com/unbraind/pm-web is unaffected by the npm scope. Reverted name back to @unbrained/pm-web. This is the one intentional scope exception in the otherwise-unscoped pm- ecosystem.
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: Hands-on functional test pass 2026-05-29 (real data + Postgres)
metadata.description: Full functional + production-hardening test pass against an isolated throwaway Postgres and real pm workspaces. Verified build/typecheck/test, both launch paths (pm web extension + node dist/server.js), and the full HTTP API (auth, projects, item CRUD, deps, search, graph, stats) with persistence cross-checked via the pm CLI. Error cases return proper 4xx. Hardened DB-config startup UX.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Functional test pass complete. Build/typecheck/test green; both launch paths verified; full HTTP API exercised with persistence cross-checked via pm CLI; error cases return proper 4xx; DB-config startup UX hardened (2 issues found+fixed).

metadata.title: Catalog npm link points to unpublished unscoped pm-web (404)
metadata.description: SYMPTOM: package.json pm.catalog.links.npm pointed to https://www.npmjs.com/package/pm-web (unscoped), which 404s. REPRO: npm view pm-web version -> E404; npm view @unbrained/pm-web version -> 2026.5.29. FINDING: the package is actually published SCOPED as @unbrained/pm-web (CI release.yml publishes require('./package.json').name with --access public; live latest = 2026.5.29). The unscoped pm-web name has never been published. Ecosystem is mixed (pm-github/pm-changelog are unscoped; pm-cli and pm-web are scoped @unbrained). EXPECTED vs OBSERVED: link should resolve to the live package; instead it 404s. RESOLUTION: did NOT rename the package to unscoped (that would orphan the live @unbrained/pm-web and break installs); fixed the dead catalog link to https://www.npmjs.com/package/@unbrained/pm-web to match the actually-published, CI-wired name.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Fixed: corrected catalog npm link to @unbrained/pm-web (the actually-published, CI-wired scoped name). Did not rename package to avoid orphaning live published package.

metadata.title: Server hangs/cryptic error when DATABASE_URL is unset
metadata.description: SYMPTOM: Starting pm-web without DATABASE_URL produced no output for several seconds, then a cryptic 'getaddrinfo EAI_AGAIN pm-telemetry-postgres' DNS error. db.ts silently fell back to a hardcoded telemetry-specific host (pm-telemetry-postgres) with undefined credentials, and the failure only surfaced after the 5s pool connectionTimeout. REPRO: env -u DATABASE_URL node dist/server.js  (observed ~5s silent wait then DNS error stack). EXPECTED: a server that requires a DB should fail fast with a clear actionable message and exit non-zero. OBSERVED vs EXPECTED: cryptic delayed DNS error vs clear 'DATABASE_URL is not set' guidance. FIX: added assertDbConfigured() in db.ts (validates DATABASE_URL or POSTGRES_HOST+POSTGRES_DB, removed the pm-telemetry-postgres default), called at the top of server.ts before any DB use; also collapsed the schema-init catch to print err.message instead of a full stack. Now: no DATABASE_URL -> instant clear message + exit 1 (1s); unreachable DB -> clean one-line timeout error + exit 1.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Fixed: assertDbConfigured() in db.ts + fast-fail in server.ts. Verified: no DATABASE_URL -> instant clear message + exit 1; unreachable DB -> clean error + exit 1; good DB -> server runs and full API works.

metadata.title: Daily Release fails at changelog:check — version-format mismatch (padded tag vs npm version)
metadata.description: Root cause: release.yml 'decide' step emits tag=v2026.05.30 (zero-padded) but npm_version=2026.5.30 (npm-stripped). 'Generate changelog' rendered the heading from the padded tag (## 2026.05.30) while 'Run release checks'->changelog:check validates with --release-version-from-package (## 2026.5.30), so the check reported 'out of date' and the scheduled Daily Release went red 4 days running. pm-web is the only package with an unreleased pm item rendered under a version heading, so it was the only one to trip it. Fix: changelog generation in release.yml now uses --release-version-from-package (identical version source to the check), so they can never diverge regardless of tag padding. Verified by full local CI simulation (release:check rc=0, heading ## 2026.5.30).
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: Add publish retry + provenance fallback to release workflow
metadata.description: Replace the npm publish step in .github/workflows/release.yml with a resilient version that (1) skips if the version is already published, (2) retries up to 3 times with 30s sleep, and (3) falls back to publishing without --provenance after persistent registry 404s. Permanent fix for the 2026-05-27 npm E404 outage that left all 19 packages tagged in git but unpublished on npm.
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: Production-readiness audit 2026-05-28
metadata.author: codex
metadata.acceptance_criteria: pm-web uses the current pm CLI and SDK package expectations, generated CHANGELOG.md matches a complete remote-tag history, release-readiness checks pass locally, package tracking records the verification evidence, and the follow-up push gives GitHub CI a clean run on the corrected tree.
metadata.resolution: pm-web was refreshed for the current pm package release line, the full-tag changelog mismatch was corrected upstream, local release checks pass on the corrected tree, and the follow-up tracking commit will exercise GitHub CI without skip markers.
metadata.expected_result: pm-web remains installable as a pm package, generated changelog output is deterministic in fresh clones with all tags, local release checks pass, pm validation succeeds with only pre-existing tracker hygiene warnings, and GitHub CI has a non-skipped run to validate the corrected tree.
metadata.actual_result: npm run release:check passed after fetching full remote tags and fast-forwarding to the corrected changelog; pm --path .agents/pm --json validate returned ok=true with known tracker hygiene warnings; the audit evidence is recorded on pm-web-8u17 for the non-skipped CI-triggering commit.
metadata.close_reason: Release-readiness audit completed with local verification and CI follow-up prepared

metadata.title: Fix pm-web startup: drop private-infra DB host default + graceful Postgres-unreachable guidance
metadata.description:
pm web crashed on startup for any non-internal user: POSTGRES_HOST defaulted to the private infra hostname 'pm-telemetry-postgres' (getaddrinfo EAI_AGAIN), and DB-init failures dumped a raw stack trace then exit 1.

Changes:
- src/db.ts: default POSTGRES_HOST -> 127.0.0.1 (was 'pm-telemetry-postgres'). Production deploy is unaffected: docker-compose.yml sets POSTGRES_HOST=pm-telemetry-postgres explicitly.
- src/server.ts: on connection-class errors (ECONNREFUSED/EAI_AGAIN/ENOTFOUND/ETIMEDOUT/28P01/28000/3D000) print actionable setup guidance (DATABASE_URL / POSTGRES_* / docker one-liner / README pointer) and exit 1 cleanly instead of a raw stack trace.

Verification (real data):
- typecheck + build green; npm test 2/2 pass.
- Unreachable DB -> friendly guidance shown, no stack trace.
- Real postgres:16 in Docker -> server boots, /healthz 200, SPA 200, user register+login (JWT issued, gen_random_uuid ok), all 7 schema tables auto-created.
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: Remove obsolete notify-website-sync workflow (dispatched deleted companion deploy.yml -> 422)
metadata.description: notify-website-sync.yml fired on every push to main and POSTed to unbraind/pm-cli-companion's deploy.yml workflow_dispatch. That deploy.yml was removed in companion commit 3c6439a ('website repo must not use GitHub runners'), so the dispatch returned HTTP 422 and the check was permanently red. The live site (pm-cli.unbrained.dev) self-refreshes its data every 5 min at runtime, so this notify->deploy chain is fully redundant. Removed the workflow; pm-web push checks are now CI + Generate Changelog + Daily Release only.
metadata.author: codex

metadata.title: Fix notify-website-sync: drop misleading source_repo alias
metadata.description: pm-web's notify-website-sync.yml was dispatching {source_repo: unbraind/pm-web, source_ref: <web-sha>} to the companion's deploy.yml. The companion treats source_repo/source_ref as legacy aliases for pm_cli_source_repo/pm_cli_source_ref, so the docs build then tried to fetch pm-cli docs (e.g. docs/QUICKSTART.md) from pm-web instead, hitting HTTP 404 for all of them and producing only 7/19 partials before the verify step failed (test -f website/index.html). Fix: only send pm_web_source_repo/pm_web_source_ref in the dispatch payload.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Fixed: notify-website-sync.yml now only sends pm_web_source_repo/pm_web_source_ref. The source_repo/source_ref aliases were causing the companion docs build to fetch pm-cli docs from pm-web (HTTP 404 across docs/*.md).

metadata.title: ci: fix release workflow step ordering
metadata.description: Swap changelog regeneration before release:check so changelog:check sees the version-bumped CHANGELOG.md instead of failing the gate.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Swapped changelog generation before release checks so changelog:check sees updated file. Verified workflow dispatch then triggers npm publish + GH release.

metadata.title: Scope npm package as @unbrained/pm-web
metadata.description: Original unscoped 'pm-web' name blocked by npm with 403: too similar to existing 'pmweb'. Switched to @unbrained scope so npm publish succeeds. Published @unbrained/pm-web@1.0.0 manually to claim the name. Manifest name (pm extension id) remains 'pm-web' so pm install paths are unchanged.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Scoped name published as @unbrained/pm-web@1.0.0; package.json updated. Daily release workflow will publish subsequent versions to the scoped name automatically.

metadata.title: Add bun-install verification to release workflow

metadata.title: Remove hardcoded personal email default from pm-web source
metadata.description: Security/privacy fix: src/db.ts, src/routes/auth.ts, sql/schema.sql had redacted@example.invalid as default for PM_WEB_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL. Replaced with empty default; guard SQL to skip promotion when env var unset. README updated. Source-only fix per user instruction (no history rewrite).
metadata.author: codex
metadata.close_reason: Source scrub complete: redacted@example.invalid removed from source, dist, sql, README updated. Verified no remaining hits.

metadata.title: Release readiness hardening for pm-web
metadata.description: Prepare pm-web as a production-ready pm package with package-local pm tracking, generated changelog data, CI and daily release automation.
metadata.author: codex
metadata.acceptance_criteria: Package installs with pm install; npm release gate passes; changelog is generated from package-local pm data; tracked files and reachable git history have no secret pattern hits.
